title: Vernonia glabra
taxonomy: Vernonia glabra is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Vernonia glabra is classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. The genus Vernonia itself is a part of the tribe Vernonieae, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Vernonia glabra is a species within the genus, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's characteristics and how it has evolved over time.
uses: Vernonia glabra is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare medicinal remedies, such as teas and infusions. It is said to be effective in treating skin conditions, wounds, and fever. Additionally, Vernonia glabra is believed to have antiseptic properties that help in preventing infections. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and other health problems in traditional medicine.

side_effects: Vernonia glabra is a plant that has been traditionally used in various folk medicines, but there is limited information available on its side effects. intro: Vernonia glabra, also known as Smooth Ironweed, is a perennial plant species native to the subtropical regions of Asia and the Americas, characterized by its hairy stems and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vernonia glabra include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Vernonia glabra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as arthritis, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
